converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Richard Schaap papers
Schaap, Dick, 1934-2001.
3036
11.4 cubic feet. 11.4 cubic feet.
[1962-1969?]. Research notes, newspaper clippings, tape recordings and typed transcripts, manuscripts, and other materials. Includes research notes, newspaper clippings, tape recordings and typed transcriptions, drafts of articles and books, and related material from Schaap's career as an author and editor. Also includes material regarding drug addiction and research material concerning Robert F. Kennedy. Collection material in English 2068274
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, April 2003
Date modified:
RMC Staff, August 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Schaap was born in New York City on September 27, 1934. He earned a B.S. from Cornell University in 1955 and an M.S. in journalism from Columbia University in 1956. He was sports editor, then general editor, then senior editor for Newsweek from 1956-1963, and in 1964 became city editor of the New York Herald Tribune. He wrote articles for Sports Illustrated, the Saturday Evening Post, and other magazines. His books include Mickey Mantle - Indispensable Yankee; Paul Hornung - Pro Football's Golden Boy; An Illustrated History of the Olympics; Instant Replay (about Jerry Kramer); RFK; and Turned On. He also edited autobiographies of Joe Namath, Frank Beard, and Tom Seaver.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes research notes, newspaper clippings, tape recordings and typed transcriptions, drafts of articles and books, and related material from Schaap's career as an author and editor. Also includes material regarding drug addiction, and research material concerning Robert F. Kennedy.
Access Restrictions:
Access restricted to the permission of the regional history curator.
Restrictions on use:
Reproductions require the permission of the regional history curator and can be used only for research purposes. Copyright is retained by donor.
Cite As:
Richard Schaap papers, #3036. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Conditions Governing Use
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.
Conditions Governing Use
Audio visual media that has been digitized: TR-18979, TR-18980, TR-18982, TR-18983.
Sportswriters. Editors. Authors. Drug addiction. Drug abuse. Sports journalism. Sports. Beard, Frank. Hornung, Paul, 1935- Kennedy, Robert F., 1925-1968. Kramer, Jerry, 1936- Mantle, Mickey, 1931-1995. Namath, Joe Willie, 1943- Seaver, Tom, 1944-2020 Schaap, Dick, 1934-2001. (Title of work: Illustrated history of the Olympics..) Scope and Contents
Notes, writings, and other information on Robert Kennedy.Used and unused tapes 1962 (small box)102
Scope and Contents
Audio tapes from interviews. Some of the names include Sterling Moss and Steve McQueen at Sebring, Jim Taylor, Ralph Nelson.
